tasks.title: Mood Ring refresh pipeline — auto-generate behavioral data + add understanding/reflecting tags
tasks.description:
## Mood Ring Refresh Pipeline

### What
Automate mood-ring.json generation and expand behavioral categories to include **understanding** and elevate **reflecting**. The Mood Ring should reflect who Jeff wants to be, not just report what's dominant.

### Current state
- mood-ring.json is static, manually generated via SQL on ~/.chorus/index.db
- 6 categories: directing (51%), reflecting (15%), questioning (11%), pushback (10%), friction (9%), deciding (4%)
- Displayed as concentric ring around Self node on mind map with tooltip bar chart

### Changes needed

**New category: Understanding**
- Pattern: messages where Jeff is synthesizing, connecting dots, building mental models
- Signal words: "I see", "so basically", "that makes sense", "the connection is", "what I hear is", "I think I understand"
- Distinct from reflecting (looking inward) — understanding is integrating external input

**Elevate Reflecting**
- Currently 15% — Jeff values this mode but defaults to directing under pressure (HBDI insight)
- Consider: weight reflecting higher in the "recent" window, or add a "reflection ratio" metric
- The ring color should shift when reflecting increases — positive feedback loop

**Automation**
- Script to regenerate mood-ring.json from chorus index (session close hook or cron)
- Incremental: only re-scan messages since last generation
- Output to jeff-bridwell-personal-site/public/mood-ring.json (bind-mounted, no deploy needed)

### Done when
- [ ] Understanding category added with pattern matching
- [ ] mood-ring.json auto-generates (not manually run)
- [ ] Ring color responds to shifts in reflecting/understanding
- [ ] Jeff sees the ring change over time and it means something to him

### Links
- Gathering card #81 (shipped the initial Mood Ring)
- DEC-031 (product vocabulary)
- Jeff HBDI insight: under pressure becomes more directing, less reflecting

tasks.title: Werk scorecard — regular maturity refresh for the nervous system
Evolutionary architecture fitness functions — catalog and track
The Clearing — where Jeff interacts with Chorus and learns from it
tasks.description:
## The Clearing (die Lichtung)

Named after Heidegger's concept of the clearing — the open space where things become visible. Not a cockpit (one-way control). Not a dashboard (passive monitoring). A bidirectional space where Jeff directs the team AND the system reflects patterns back.

### What happens in The Clearing
- **Jeff directs**: board walks, priority calls, naming decisions, vision work
- **The system reflects**: Mood Ring (behavioral patterns), reconciliation output, cost tracking, maturity scorecards
- **Roles respond**: filtered views of what each role said/committed/shipped
- **Decisions land**: the place where direction becomes recorded decision

### Design principles
- Bidirectional: not just control, but learning and reflection
- The Clearing is within the Werk (Gathering as life's work) — the space where the work becomes visible
- Lightweight: Jeff should be able to enter The Clearing from anywhere (desk, phone, garden)
- Consolidates: /chorus output, board state, mood ring, cost burn, maturity scorecard — all in one interaction space

### Prior art
- Mood Ring shipped (#81) — behavioral analysis visualization on Self node
- /chorus skill — shared memory query for session continuity
- Chorus Activity Dashboard (Grafana) — operational view
- Board scripts (board.sh, chorus-board.sh) — kanban interaction

### What this is NOT
- Not a new app or separate UI (yet)
- Not a replacement for 1x1 sessions with roles
- Not just Grafana dashboards relabeled

### Open questions
- Is The Clearing a page in Gathering, a CLI experience, or both?
- How does the Mood Ring feed into The Clearing vs. living on the mind map?
- What's the minimum version that's useful?

Jeff's words: "This is a place where I interact with the Chorus and learn from it as well."

Naming: Jeff chose The Clearing over Cockpit, Dashboard, Hearth, Score. Werk (das Werk) held separately — that's the word for Gathering as life's work, not a feature name.

tasks.title: Chorus static page — doc links + team activity view
tasks.description:
## What
Chorus landing page at /chorus — project-level page in Cultivating nav, peer to Light Life.

## Why
Gives Jeff a single entry point to see Chorus as a product: team structure, value stream diagrams, shared memory links, core documents, and live team activity.

## Done criteria (all met)
- [x] /chorus route renders with gathering.css
- [x] Nav: moved from Admin to Cultivating dropdown
- [x] 8 Mermaid diagrams covering value stream, ownership, interaction modes, security
- [x] Shared Memory section: context service, both Vikunja boards, Grafana dashboard
- [x] Core Documents, ADRs, Operational sections with doc links
- [x] Team table (4 roles)
- [x] Live Loki activity feed with role/component filters + auto-refresh

## Links
- Brief: engineer/briefs/2026-02-19-chorus-page.md
- View: views/chorus.ejs
- Nav: views/partials/navbar.ejs

Data scrubbing enforcement — bridge context + memory write scrubbers

Team communication ring — with/at/over detection from shared memory
Concentric trust model — pod-mediated coordination + local AI for Self
Session templates — interaction protocols for Demo, Board Walk, Review, SWAT
tasks.description:
## Session Templates — Interaction Protocols

### What
Formalize session types as interaction protocols with a speed/detail slider. Each type calibrates response length, turn-taking, and depth. Wren reads intent and sets the dial — Jeff never types a command prefix.

### Speed/Detail Slider

| Type | Speed | Detail | Response limit | Turns |
|------|-------|--------|---------------|-------|
| Standup | High | Low | 1 sentence/role | 1 round |
| Demo | Med | Med | 3 sentences/role, moderated | 2-3 rounds |
| Board walk | Med | Low | Status only, depth on request | Card by card |
| Review | Low | High | Full analysis, trade-offs | Open |
| SWAT | High | High | Precise triage, no filler | Until resolved |
| 1x1 | Jeff pace | Jeff pull | Match his energy | He leads |

### Design principles
- **No prefixes**: Jeff talks naturally, Wren detects session type from intent
- **Wren moderates**: Sets the dial, controls turns, captures decisions
- **Override anytime**: Jeff says "go deeper" or "speed up" and the dial moves
- **Brevity default**: Slack posts 50 words max regardless of session type
- **Depth in briefs**: If a role needs to say more, write a brief — don't flood the channel

### How it works
1. Jeff says something ("walk me through the boards" / "what did Kade ship?")
2. Wren recognizes the session type and sets speed/detail
3. Roles respond within the constraint
4. Wren captures decisions and routes commitments
5. Shared memory indexes the exchange

### Implementation
- Session type detection logic in Wren CLAUDE.md (moderator protocol)
- Response constraints in all three CLAUDE.md files (Slack brevity rules)
- Bridge change: auto-route Jeff messages in #all-gathering (no @team required)
- Commitment capture: already shipped (commitment-brief-writer.ts)

### Done when
- [ ] Wren CLAUDE.md has moderator protocol with session type detection
- [ ] All CLAUDE.md files have Slack brevity rules (50 word max)
- [ ] Bridge routes Jeff messages without @team prefix
- [ ] One successful moderated demo that Jeff says felt smooth
- [ ] Speed/detail slider feels natural, not mechanical

### Jeff feedback driving this
- "Prefixing everything with @team feels artificial and makes me type more"
- "The noise and experience of the meeting is so choppy my mood shifts"
- "I have spent time normalizing in the code more and we need to normalize here"
- Speed/detail slider idea is Jeff's — calibrate per session type, not one-size-fits-all
